SPIAT3D 0.1.0

Calculate mixing scores gradient on 3D spatial data.

Source: R/calculate_mixing_scores_gradient3D.R
calculate_mixing_scores_gradient3D.Rd

This function calculates the mixing scores gradient on a 3D SpatialExperiment Object. See paper on theory behind mixing scores gradient (I ain't explaining it here).

calculate_mixing_scores_gradient3D(
  spe,
  reference_cell_type,
  target_cell_type,
  radii,
  feature_colname = "Cell.Type",
  plot_image = TRUE
)

Arguments

spe

A SpatialExperiment object containing 3D spatial information for the cells. Naming of spatial coordinates MUST be "Cell.X.Position", "Cell.Y.Position", "Cell.Z.Position" for the x-coordinate, y-coordinate and z-coordinate of each cell.

reference_cell_type

A string specifying the reference cell type.

target_cell_type

A string specifying the target cell type.

radii

A positive, ascending numeric vector specifying the set of radius values used.

feature_colname

A string specifying the name of the column in the colData slot of the SpatialExperiment object that contains the cell type information. Defaults to "Cell.Type".

plot_image

A logical indicating whether to plot mixing scores gradient as a line graph. Defaults to TRUE.

Value

A data frame containing mixing score values and associated information across each radii (rows).

Examples

# Get simulated SpatialExperiment object to use as an example for analysis
simulated_spe <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "simulated_spe.rds", package = "SPIAT3D"))

result <- calculate_mixing_scores_gradient3D(
    spe = simulated_spe,
    reference_cell_type = "Tumour",
    target_cell_type = "Immune",
    radii = seq(20, 100, 10),
    feature_colname = "Cell.Type",
    plot_image = TRUE
)
